errors_found_in_summary.summary_claim: RE-505 retracted because 'weekend log suppression spec' explains absence of unified_runner.20260530.* file; therefore main.py standalone production hypothesis is invalidated
actual_evidence: unified_runner.20260529.stderr.log spans 2026-05-29T01:26:29Z through 2026-05-31T10:10:26Z — UnifiedRunner ran CONTINUOUSLY across 5/30 (grep for '2026-05-30' inside this file yields 32795 entries, including '2026-05-30T00:00:51Z' candle data fetch entries)

Therefore the 'weekend log absence' premise is FACTUALLY WRONG — UnifiedRunner DID log on 5/30, just under the 20260529 file rotation key (because process was launched 5/29 and not restarted until 5/31 10:10)
The audit log audit_2026-05-30.jsonl contains 2 kill_switch_activated entries at 02:49:32 and 07:48:05 UTC plus 20 ptrc_rejected entries for ATLAS-2026-0424-001 with available_margin=1000000
grep 'kill_switch_activated' in unified_runner.20260529.stderr.log: 0 matches. grep 'ptrc_rejected' in same file: 2 matches (5/29 dates only, none on 5/30)

Stronger evidence on 5/31: unified_runner.20260531.stderr.log is logging continuously starting 10:10:30Z. audit_2026-05-31.jsonl line 1 shows kill_switch_activated at 2026-05-31T23:21:50.023708Z + 15 ptrc_rejected entries at 23:21:55+ with same available_margin=1000000 pattern. grep 'kill_switch_activated|ptrc_rejected' in unified_runner.20260531.stderr.log: 0 matches

5/31 23:21 UTC is AFTER Sunday 22:00 UTC market reopen — the CLAUDE.md weekend suppression spec explicitly applies only to 'Saturday 22:00 UTC - Sunday 22:00 UTC' window. UnifiedRunner was actively logging candle fetches at 23:20:17Z (proven by grep). So suppression cannot explain absence of these events in stderr

Conclusion: The audit_<date>.jsonl entries on 5/30 02:49-07:48 and 5/31 23:21 CANNOT have originated from the UnifiedRunner process whose stderr we see. They must originate from a SECOND process (main.py with PA-103 escape hatch) OR from a different runner instance
errors_found_in_summary.verification_method: Read all three audit_log files (5/29-5/31), grep stderr logs for matching events, cross-verify UnifiedRunner running window vs missing event types
errors_found_in_summary.impact: RE-505 retraction in summary is unsafe. The CC-001 compound risk (main.py standalone + PA-503 detection dead) is NOT actually refuted by the weekend spec. The summary should re-instate RE-505 as HIGH (recommend CRITICAL) and reopen CC-001
errors_found_in_summary.summary_correction_required: Re-classify RE-505 as HIGH (not INVALIDATED). CC-001 must be re-opened. The weekend log suppression spec is REAL but does not explain this specific anomaly — both KS activations are well outside the documented suppression window OR are accompanied by direct stderr evidence that UnifiedRunner was running and not emitting these events

actual_facts: Item count listed = 10 (PA-503, PA-505, PA-506, RE-501, RE-502, RE-503, RE-504, DE-501, DE-505, DM-501) but the lead-in says '6 件の HIGH'

PA-505 and PA-506 are MEDIUM in summary (lines 105-107)

RE-505.original_verdict_in_summary: INVALIDATED via weekend log suppression spec
RE-505.reverification_verdict: INVALIDATION_REJECTED — finding stands at HIGH (CRITICAL upgrade arguable)
evidence_against_invalidation: (1) unified_runner.20260529.stderr.log covers 5/30 with 32795 log entries (NOT suppressed). Audit log kill_switch_activated on 5/30 02:49 has 0 matching entries in this file. The audit log entry must have come from a DIFFERENT process

(2) unified_runner.20260531.stderr.log is active continuously starting 10:10:30Z including 23:20:17Z candle fetch. Audit log kill_switch_activated at 23:21:50Z has 0 matching entries in this file. 5/31 23:21 UTC is AFTER Sunday 22:00 UTC reopen — outside the documented suppression window

(3) In UnifiedRunner code path: PortfolioSnapshot.available_margin = account_info.available_margin (risk_supervisor.py:862) — comes from OANDA broker ~298k. The 1_000_000 default ONLY exists in settings.py:130 (PlatformSettings) and coordinator.py:62 (Coordinator parameter), both used by main.py path. UnifiedRunner cannot produce available_margin=1000000 because account_info.balance fallback is 300_000.0 (strategy_slot.py:540)

(4) Therefore: the audit log entries demonstrably came from a non-UnifiedRunner process. Hypothesis: main.py running with PLATFORM_ALLOW_MAIN_PRODUCTION_LIVE escape hatch (likely in dev/research mode, not literally LIVE) or a stale dev-side coordinator process
RE-505.weakened_part_of_original_finding: RE-505 originally suggested 'main.py standalone production' but the simpler hypothesis is 'main.py running concurrently with UnifiedRunner, possibly in paper or research mode, with default 1M balance'. Either way, dual runner risk is real
RE-505.criticism: summary's retraction relied on misinterpretation. The week-end log suppression spec is REAL (CLAUDE.md confirmed) but does not cover the specific time windows of these audit entries

additional_observations.topic: UnifiedRunner does NOT write ptrc_rejected to audit log
additional_observations.finding: strategy_slot.py:658 emits logger.warning('ptrc_rejected') to stderr only. It does NOT call audit_logger.record(). The audit log entries come solely from ptrc._fire_ptrc_audit() module-level helper which uses the global _audit_logger set in runner.py:487.
additional_observations.implication: Since _fire_ptrc_audit IS called from ptrc.evaluate() (line 281, 321, 402), audit log entries SHOULD appear when UnifiedRunner processes a PTRC rejection. The 0-match in unified_runner.20260529.stderr.log strongly suggests the PTRC evaluations on 5/30 02:49 did NOT go through UnifiedRunner at all. This further strengthens the dual-runner hypothesis.

additional_observations.finding: 5/30/2026 is Saturday. Per CLAUDE.md weekend suppression spec, OANDA market closes Saturday 22:00 UTC. The 02:49 UTC and 07:48 UTC events on 5/30 are 14h+ BEFORE the closure window. They cannot be suppressed by this spec.
additional_observations.implication: The summary's retraction conflates 'weekend' (Sat-Sun calendar) with the specific 24h suppression window 'Sat 22:00 - Sun 22:00 UTC'. These are different concepts.

additional_observations.topic: DE-505 (now folded into DE-501) fix touched alerting_rules.yml
additional_observations.finding: git diff shows deploy/prometheus/alerting_rules.yml updated to mark DE-503 ADVISORY as 'DE-501 修正済'. This may require operator awareness to re-verify alerting routing
PA-506 finding claims 'StrategyLifecycle 参照は本モジュール内のみ (1 ファイル)' but tests/unit/test_phase3_platform.py imports it
additional_observations.implication: Substantively correct (no PRODUCTION wiring) but textual claim is slightly imprecise. Suggest tightening to 'no production wiring; only unit test in test_phase3_platform.py'

conclusion.fixes_quality: HIGH — PA-501/502/DE-501 are correctly applied and adequately regression-tested
conclusion.summary_quality: MIXED — most findings are accurately documented and severity-classified, but the RE-505/CC-001 retraction is unsafe and based on a misapplication of the weekend log suppression spec
conclusion.main_recommendation: REOPEN RE-505 at HIGH (CRITICAL recommended) and CC-001. Conduct file-locking based exclusive startup check (RE-505 recommendation c) immediately. Investigate why audit_log entries exist on dates/times when UnifiedRunner stderr proves it was running but emitted no matching events — this is the smoking gun for a second writer process.
conclusion.verification_completeness: Time-boxed within 20 min budget. PA-501/502/DE-501 fixes verified in code and tests. PA-503/RE-501/RE-508 high-severity findings re-confirmed by grep. RE-505 retraction empirically refuted. Items not re-verified due to scope: RE-506 (recent stderr inspection), parts of RE-511 (cancel_open_orders failure paths).
